comment_text.sn6.9:1.2: His illness was caused by slandering Sāriputta and Moggallāna (SN 6.10, AN 10.89, Snp 3.10:3.3).| AN 10.89 combines this Sutta and the next.
comment_text.sn6.9:3.3: The ancient Indian game of dice involved casting vibhītaka seeds (also known as “bedda nuts”, from Terminalia bellirica) in a hollow, from which the players would take a handful. A number divisible by four was “perfect” (kaṭa), so the fifth seed meant a “losing” hand (kali; cf. apaṇṇaka in MN 60 and notes).
comment_text.sn6.9:5.1: The interpretation and calculation of these lines is explained at Snp 3.10:12.2.

root_text.sn6.9:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena kokāliko bhikkhu ābādhiko hoti dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.sn6.9:1.3: Atha kho turū paccekabrahmā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇo kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena kokāliko bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā vehāsaṁ ṭhito kokālikaṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.9:1.8: “Nanu tvaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā anāgāmī byākato, atha kiñcarahi idhāgato?

translation_text.sn6.9:1.2: Now at that time the mendicant Kokālika was sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn6.9:1.3: Then, late at night, the glorious independent divinity Tudu, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the mendicant Kokālika, and standing in the air he said to him,
translation_text.sn6.9:1.4: “Kokālika, have confidence in Sāriputta and Moggallāna,
translation_text.sn6.9:1.8: “Didn’t the Buddha declare you a non-returner? So what then are you doing back here?
translation_text.sn6.9:1.9: See how far you have strayed!”
translation_text.sn6.9:2.2: with an axe in his mouth.
translation_text.sn6.9:2.3: A fool cuts themselves with it
translation_text.sn6.9:3.1: When you praise someone worthy of criticism,
translation_text.sn6.9:3.2: or criticize someone worthy of praise,
translation_text.sn6.9:3.3: you choose a losing hand with your own mouth:
translation_text.sn6.9:3.4: you’ll never find happiness that way.
translation_text.sn6.9:4.1: A losing hand at dice is a trivial thing,
translation_text.sn6.9:4.2: if all you lose is your money
translation_text.sn6.9:4.3: and all you own, even yourself.
translation_text.sn6.9:4.5: is to hate the holy ones.
translation_text.sn6.9:5.1: For a hundred thousand times a hundred million,
translation_text.sn6.9:5.2: times five hundred and thirty-six times <j>a thousand times ten million years
translation_text.sn6.9:5.3: a slanderer of noble ones goes to hell,
translation_text.sn6.9:5.4: having aimed bad words and thoughts at them.”

variant_text.sn6.9:0.3: Turūbrahmasutta → tudubrahmasuttaṁ (bj, sya-all, pts2ed); tudu brahmā (pts1ed)
variant_text.sn6.9:1.3: turū → tudu (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
variant_text.sn6.9:5.3: Yamariyagarahī → yamariye garahī (sya-all, km); yamariyaṁ garahaṁ (mr)
